news.title: 2026 World Cup: All the European sides' results
news.lede: Look back at all the European sides' results at the FIFA World Cup in Canada, Mexico and the United States. The 2026 FIFA World Cup in Canada, Mexico and the United States kicked off on 11 June and concluded with Spain's extra-time final victory against holders Argentina in East Rutherford on 19 July. Check out all the details of the European teams' matches. Wednesday 17 June: W 3-1 vs Jordan (San Francisco Bay Area) Monday 22 June: L 0-2 vs Argentina (Dallas) Sunday 28 June: D 3-3 vs Algeria (Kansas City) Thursday 2 July (R32): L 3-0 vs Spain (Los Angeles).

news.title: Netflix to continue ‘The Rest is Football’ podcast post-World Cup
news.lede: After a modestly successful run during the World Cup, it appears as if Netflix is trying to stay top of mind for soccer fans. On Monday, Netflix committed to continuing The Rest is Football podcast beyond the World Cup. The streamer created the daily show, hosted by BBC’s former Match of the Day presenter Gary Lineker, for the World Cup. The show will continue throughout at least the next two Premier League seasons, airing two episodes per week, according to a report by Max Goldbart in Deadline.

news.title: Football Extra: An incredible World Cup – almost until the end
news.lede: It was a an incredible World Cup, but sadly you cannot add the word 'Final' to that phrase. You probably had to be Spanish or more specifically a Spanish football fan to truly enjoy the120 minutes that were frequently tedious for the billions watching who aren't true football aficionados. How could this happen after such a tournament. I don't think it was difficult to predict basically because I did exactly that in my last Newsletter, 'It should be an interesting game.

news.title: Three Observations from Spain’s 1-0 win over Argentina in the 2026 World Cup final
news.lede: Let’s be honest, the semifinals were better This always happens with finals. It was the same in the UEFA Champions League. You get safe, boring, careful football. The semifinals have lower stakes, but they deliver better thrills.

news.title: 2026 World Cup: Spain 1-0 Argentina (AET): Ferran Torres' extra-time winner secures Spain's second title
news.lede: Spain won their second World Cup title after Ferran Torres' 106th-minute smashed finish earned a 1-0 win against defending champions Argentina in the 2026 final. Spain were deserved winners. Torres scored with Spain's 20th shot while the game ended without Argentina registering a single shot on target. Lionel Messi touched the ball just 54 times in a match that took three hours to complete and featured a 27-minute half-time break.

news.title: Spain secure World Cup glory to banish miserable Argentina and their anti-football
news.lede: Spain 1-0 Argentina (after extra time): Ferran Torres finally buried the 10 men of Argentina in extra-time after a turgid display from Lionel Messi and his teammates in New York. From reproductive rights to climate change to Big Tech, The Independent is on the ground when the story is developing. Whether it's investigating the financials of Elon Musk's pro-Trump PAC or producing our latest documentary, 'The A Word', which shines a light on the American women fighting for reproductive rights, we know how important it is to parse out the facts from the messaging. At such a critical moment in US history, we need reporters on the ground.

news.title: World Cup final - Spain 1-0 Argentina: Lionel Messi's side defended by national newspapers despite dismal display
news.lede: Argentina's national media defended their team's performance in the World Cup final - despite a defensive and ill-disciplined display. Lionel Messi's side barely made the game with Spain a contest. They did not have a shot in normal time - nor an effort on target in the entire 120 minutes. At full-time, Leandro Paredes, who arguably should have joined Fernandez in being sent off for two yellow cards, was given his marching orders for his angry response at the whistle.

news.title: At the World Cup of superstars, a team take home the trophy | Sid Lowe
news.lede: The European champions conceded just one goal in the tournament and Spain’s success is a victory for a system. Release the pressure, let it out. All of it and all of them. Again and again they had come, again and again they had been halted but they would not be denied.
